integration_validation_v2.evidence: request_replay comfy.rs:2907-2953 emits run-scoped REPLAY_REQUESTED/COMPLETED/FAILED, proven by atelier_replay_contract_tests.rs:162-249. Contract also requires queue/run/result + registration success/failure-recovery families: JOB_* (comfy.rs:110-120, enqueue at ~3259) and record_comfy_output_registration_failure/retry (comfy.rs:2285,2442) are REAL code but NOT exercised by U20's cited test, so this unit only proves the replay subset -- partial proof, non-blocking.

integration_validation_v3.evidence: V2 gap was unexercised JOB_* + registration-recovery families. New tests/atelier_comfy_job_registration_event_families_pg_tests.rs:40-191 mt130 (57449ac7): enqueue_comfy_job -> mark_running -> mark_completed with PG re-read of status/timestamps, per-aggregate event counts ==1 for JOB_ENQUEUED/JOB_RUNNING/JOB_COMPLETED, registration-failure record -> retry with re-read of Registered/resolved_intake_output_id/retry_count, OUTPUT_REGISTRATION_FAILURE_RECORDED/RETRIED counts. All 8 contract families asserted in event_family::ALL. Replay subset already proven run-scoped in atelier_replay_contract_tests.rs:162-249.
integration_validation_v3.basis: v3 adversarial per-MT review of remediated deliverable + runtime proof via full-crate managed-PG sweep and kernel-builder fresh-DB proof logs

minimum_controls: Use contract-first identity and preserve projection hash.
risks: Imported MT contract may under-capture prose-only review or proof instructions.

acceptance_criteria: MT-130 [Pose-ComfyUI-Pipeline] ComfyUI Replay And Registration Event Families -- Emit EventLedger/Flight Recorder events for ComfyUI queue/run/result
proof_targets: cargo test + runtime proof vs PostgreSQL/EventLedger
scope.risk_if_missed: CKC parity loss for ComfyUI Replay And Registration Event Families
scope.summary: MT-130 [Pose-ComfyUI-Pipeline] ComfyUI Replay And Registration Event Families -- Emit EventLedger/Flight Recorder events for ComfyUI queue/run/result, replay requested/completed/failed, and output registration success/failure recovery.
